The hot-path\n94\t                       -- component lookup keys on this column, no LLM (see UI-Regen Agent /\n95\t                       -- component_usage_patterns).\n96\t    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n97\t    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n98\t    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n99\t    acked_at topics(last_active DESC);\n131\t\n132\t-- Topic context cache: pre-warmed context for active topics\n133\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS topic_context_cache (\n134\t    topic_id     TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n135\t    context_data TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: pre-fetched context (kubectl, git, beads results)\n136\t    fetched_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n137\t    expires_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n138\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n139\t);\n140\t\n141\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_context_expires ON topic_context_cache(expires_at);\n142\t\n143\t-- Intent-topic many-to-many relationship\n144\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS intent_topics (\n145\t    intent_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n146\t    topic_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n147\t    PRIMARY KEY (intent_id, topic_id),\n148\t    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n149\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE\n150\t);\n151\t\n152\t-- Feedback signals: implicit user behavior tracking for background analysis\n153\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS feedback_signals (\n154\t    signal_id    TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n155\t    signal_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n156\t    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n157\t    result_id    TEXT,\n158\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n159\t    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n160\t    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n161\t    surface_type TEXT,\n162\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n163\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n164\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n165\t    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n166\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n167\t);\n168\t\n169\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n170\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n171\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n172\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n173\t\n174\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n175\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n176\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n177\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n178\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n179\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n180\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n181\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n182\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n183\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n184\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n185\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n186\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n187\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n188\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n189\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n190\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n191\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n192\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n193\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n194\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n195\t);\n196\t\n197\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n198\t\"\"\"\n199\t\n200\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n201\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n202\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n203\t    \"router_ms\",\n204\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n205\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n206\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n207\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n208\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n209\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n210\t    \"stt_ms\",\n211\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n212\t)\n213\t\n214\t\n215\tclass SessionStore:\n216\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n217\t\n218\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n219\t        self.db_path = db_path\n220\t\n221\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n222\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n223\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n224\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n225\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n226\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n227\t\n228\t            # Create schema\n229\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n230\t            await db.commit()\n231\t\n232\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n233\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n234\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n235\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n236\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n237\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n238\t\n239\t    @staticmethod\n240\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n241\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n242\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n243\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n244\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n245\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n246\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n247\t            await db.execute(\n248\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n249\t            )\n250\t\n251\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n252\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n253\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n254\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n255\t\n256\t        await db.commit()\n257\t\n258\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n259\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n260\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n261\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n262\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n263\t\n264\t    # Session operations\n265\t    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n266\t        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n267\t        if not session_id:\n268\t            session_id = str(uuid4())\n269\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n270\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n271\t            await db.execute(\n272\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n273\t                (session_id, now, now)\n274\t            )\n275\t            await db.commit()\n276\t        return session_id\n277\t\n278\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n279\t        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n280\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n281\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n282\t            async with db.execute(\n283\t                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n284\t            ) as cursor:\n285\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n286\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n287\t\n288\t    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n289\t        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n290\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n291\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n292\t            await db.execute(\n293\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? Returns a removal summary.\n309\t\n310\t        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n311\t        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n312\t        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n313\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n314\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n315\t\n316\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n317\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n318\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n319\t\n320\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n321\t        \"\"\"\n322\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n323\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n324\t\n325\t            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n326\t            async with db.execute(\n327\t                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n328\t            ) as cur:\n329\t                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n330\t            async with db.execute(\n331\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n332\t            ) as cur:\n333\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n334\t\n335\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n336\t            await db.execute(\n337\t                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n338\t            )\n339\t            await db.execute(\n340\t                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n341\t            )\n342\t            await db.execute(\n343\t                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n344\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n345\t                (session_id,),\n346\t            )\n347\t            # dispatch_timings is keyed by the intent *thread* id\n348\t            # (routed_intent.intent_id), which differs from intents.id for the\n349\t            # current router path (see the table comment in SCHEMA_SQL), so this\n350\t            # is best-effort: it catches rows keyed by the store intent id, while\n351\t            # thread-keyd rows survive until the thread id is known to the caller.\n352\t            await db.execute(\n353\t                \"DELETE FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id IN \"\n354\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n355\t                (session_id,),\n356\t            )\n357\t            await db.execute(\n358\t                \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n359\t            )\n360\t            await db.execute(\n361\t                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n362\t            )\n363\t            await db.execute(\n364\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n365\t                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n366\t                (session_id,),\n367\t            )\n368\t            await db.execute(\n369\t                \"DELETE FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n370\t            )\n371\t            await db.execute(\n372\t                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n373\t            )\n374\t            await db.execute(\n375\t                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n376\t            )\n377\t            await db.commit()\n378\t\n379\t        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n380\t\n381\t    # Surface operations\n382\t    async def register_surface(\n383\t        self,\n384\t        session_id: str,\n385\t        surface_type: AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n680\t                (session_id,)\n681\t            ))[0][0]\n682\t\n683\t            # Get new results (unsurfaced results)\n684\t            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n685\t                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n686\t                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n687\t                (session_id,)\n688\t            ))[0][0]\n689\t\n690\t            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n691\t            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n692\t                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n693\t                   WHERE session_id = ? NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n894\t        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n895\t        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n896\t        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n897\t        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n898\t\n899\t        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n900\t        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n901\t        \"\"\"\n902\t        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n903\t\n904\t        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n905\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n906\t            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n907\t                if since is not None:\n908\t                    query = (\n909\t                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n910\t                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),  -- 1 when no component matched this result_type (or below threshold): the client renders the built-in generic fallback card (see Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card). 0 when a real component rendered it (card_cache holds the rendered HTML).\n    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n    acked_at    INTEGER,\n ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session', Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n-- first token separately) — see INTEGER,\n    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n\"\"\"\n\n# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\nDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n    \"router_ms\",\n    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n    \"escalate_ms\",\n    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n    \"stt_ms\",\n    \"first_render_ms\",\n)\n\n\nclass SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n        self.db_path = db_path\n\n    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n            )\n\n        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n\n        await db.commit()\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n\n    # Session operations\n    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        if not session_id:\n            session_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                (session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return session_id\n\n    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def delete_session(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Delete a session and every row tied to it. Returns a removal summary.\n\n        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n\n        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n\n        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n        \"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cur:\n                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cur:\n                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n\n            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            # dispatch_timings is keyed by the intent *thread* id\n            # (routed_intent.intent_id), which differs from intents.id for the\n            # current router path (see the table comment in SCHEMA_SQL), so this\n            # is best-effort: it catches rows keyed by the store intent id, while\n            # thread-keyd rows survive until the thread id is known to the caller.\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n                (session_id,),\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n\n    # Surface operations\n    async def register_surface(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n        always_available: bool = False,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Register a new surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        surface_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO surfaces\n                   (id, session_id, type, state, always_available, last_seen)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'active', ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (surface_id, session_id, surface_type, 1 if always_available else 0, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return surface_id\n\n    async def update_surface_heartbeat(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update surface last_seen timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET last_seen = ?, state = 'active' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, surface_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_surface_disconnected(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a surface as disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET state = 'disconnected' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_surfaces(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all active surfaces for a session, ordered by last_seen.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND state IN ('active', 'idle')\n                   ORDER BY last_seen DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_fallback_surface(self) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the always-available fallback surface (Telegram).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE always_available = 1 AND state != 'disconnected'\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Utterance operations\n    async def create_utterance(self, session_id: str, raw_text: str, utterance_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new utterance and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        utterance_id = utterance_id or str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO utterances (id, session_id, raw_text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n                (utterance_id, session_id, raw_text, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return utterance_id\n\n    # Intent operations\n    async def create_intent(\n        self,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n        lookup_kind: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new intent and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        intent_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO intents\n                   (id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, lookup_kind, bead_ref, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (intent_id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, lookup_kind, bead_ref, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return intent_id\n\n    async def update_intent_status(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        status: str,\n        resolved_at: int | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update intent status.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            if resolved_at is None and status == \"resolved\":\n                resolved_at = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE intents SET status = ?, resolved_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (status, resolved_at, intent_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_pending_intents(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending intents for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    # Result operations\n    async def create_result(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        previous_result_id: str | None = None,\n        diff_summary: str | None = None,\n        diff_data: dict | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new result and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        result_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO results\n                   (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, created_at, surfaced_at,\n                    previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    result_id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, json.dumps(data),\n                    urgency, now, now, previous_result_id, diff_summary,\n                    json.dumps(diff_data) if diff_data else None\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return result_id\n\n    async def get_unsurfed_results(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get results that haven't been surfaced to the user yet.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at ASC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark all unsurfed results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed_by_ids(self, session_id: str, result_ids: list[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark specific results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for result_id in result_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND session_id = ?\",\n                    (now, result_id, session_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get active topics for a session, ordered by last_active.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT t.*,\n                          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results r WHERE r.topic_id = t.id) as result_count\n                   FROM topics t\n                   WHERE (t.session_id = ? OR t.scope IN ('cross-session', 'global'))\n                     AND t.archived_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY t.last_active DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_latest_result_for_topic(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the most recent result for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE topic_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def link_intent_to_topic(self, intent_id: str, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Link an intent to a topic (many-to-many).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO intent_topics (intent_id, topic_id) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Workload summary for reconnect\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get summary of what happened while surface was disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Get pending intents\n            pending_count = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intents WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (unsurfaced results)\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (one_hour_ago,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n\n    # Feedback signal operations\n    async def create_feedback_signal(\n        self,\n        signal_id: str,\n        signal_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        result_id: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n        timestamp: int | None = None,\n        data: dict | None = None,\n        surface_type: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO feedback_signals\n                   (signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id,\n                    topic_id, timestamp or now, json.dumps(data or {}),\n                    surface_type\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return signal_id\n\n    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n            params = []\n\n            if signal_type:\n                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n                params.append(signal_type)\n\n            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n            params.append(limit)\n\n            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_signals_processed(\n        self,\n        signal_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE signal_id = ?\",\n                    (now, signal_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Dispatch timing operations (Latency Budget & Instrumentation)\n    async def record_dispatch_timings(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        **fields: int | None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Persist per-stage timings for one dispatch intent thread.\n\n        Upserts the row so the same intent_id can be written incrementally:\n        process_intent() records the server-side stages it measures, the\n        dispatch caller records sse_emit_ms after the SSE broadcast, and the\n        /api/v1/timings endpoint records client-reported stt_ms/first_render_ms.\n        Only columns passed with a non-NULL value are touched, so a later\n        partial write never clobbers a stage an earlier write already set.\n        ``created_at`` is stamped once on insert and never overwritten.\n        \"\"\"\n        cols = {\n            k: v for k, v in fields.items()\n            if k in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS and v is not None\n        }\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Ensure the row exists (created_at set once, on first write).\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dispatch_timings (intent_id, created_at) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, now),\n            )\n            if cols:\n                set_clause = \", \".join(f\"{c} = ?\" for c in cols)\n                params = [*cols.values(), intent_id]\n                await db.execute(\n                    f\"UPDATE dispatch_timings SET {set_clause} WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                    params,\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_dispatch_timings(self, intent_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the dispatch_timings row for one intent thread, or None.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id = ?\",\n                (intent_id,),\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def get_latency_percentiles(self, since: int | None = None) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Aggregate p50/p95 per stage across dispatch_timings rows.\n\n        Returns ``{stage: {\"p50\": ms, \"p95\": ms, \"count\": n}}`` for every stage\n        that has at least one non-NULL value. NULLs are skipped per stage (so a\n        stage not yet captured for any dispatch simply doesn't appear). Pass\n        ``since`` (a unix timestamp) to window to recent dispatches only — the\n        latency-baseline bead consumes the un-windowed call to fill the plan's\n        global Measured p50/p95 columns.\n\n        Percentiles are nearest-rank (ceil(q/100 * n)), computed in Python\n        because SQLite has no built-in percentile.\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..instrument.timings import percentiles\n\n        result: dict[str, dict] = {}\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for stage in DISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS:\n                if since is not None:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= ? \"\n                        f\"ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params: tuple = (since,)\n                else:\n                    query = (\n                        f\"SELECT {stage} FROM dispatch_timings \"\n                        f\"WHERE {stage} IS NOT NULL ORDER BY {stage}\"\n                    )\n                    params = ()\n                async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                    values = [row[0] for row in await cursor.fetchall()]\n                if not values:\n                    continue\n                pct = percentiles(values, qs=(50, 95))\n                result[stage] = {\n                    \"p50\": pct[50],\n                    \"p95\": pct[95],\n                    \"count\": len(values),\n                }\n        return result\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n    async def create_result_with_diff(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a new result with automatic diff computation.\n\n        Returns (result_id, has_diff).\n\n        Convenience method that automatically computes the diff against\n        the previous result for the same topic (if one exists).\n        \"\"\"\n        from ..diff.engine import get_diff_engine\n\n        # Get previous result for this topic\n        previous_result = await self.get_latest_result_for_topic(topic_id)\n\n        # Compute diff if we have a previous result\n        diff_summary = None\n        diff_data = None\n        has_diff = False\n\n        if previous_result:\n            diff_engine = get_diff_engine()\n            diff_result = await diff_engine.compute_diff(\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                previous_result=previous_result,\n                current_result={\"data\": data},\n            )\n\n            if diff_result.has_changes:\n                has_diff = True\n                diff_summary = diff_result.change_summary\n                diff_data = {\n                    \"fields\": [\n                        {\n                            \"field_name\": f.field_name,\n                            \"old_value\": f.old_value,\n                            \"new_value\": f.new_value,\n                            \"change_type\": f.change_type,\n                        }\n                        for f in diff_result.fields\n                    ],\n                    \"summary\": diff_result.summary,\n                }\n\n        # Create the result with diff data\n        result_id = await self.create_result(\n            intent_id=intent_id,\n            topic_id=topic_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            summary=summary,\n            data=data,\n            urgency=urgency,\n            previous_result_id=previous_result[\"id\"] if previous_result else None,\n            diff_summary=diff_summary,\n            diff_data=diff_data,\n        )\n\n        return result_id, has_diff\n\n\n# Global session store instance\n_store: SessionStore | None = None\n\n\ndef get_store(db_path: Path | None = None) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global session store instance.\n\n    When db_path is not given, resolves in this order:\n      1. ADC_DB_PATH env var (used by tests to isolate from production data)\n      2. DEFAULT_DB_PATH (data/session.db)\n    \"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        if db_path is None:\n            env_path = os.environ.get(\"ADC_DB_PATH\")\n            db_path = Path(env_path) if env_path else DEFAULT_DB_PATH\n        _store = SessionStore(db_path)\n    return _store\n","numLines":1054,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1054}}}

It is passed through the router\n(``route_utterance`` → ``process_intent`` → the fetch/synthesize/escalate\nbranches); each stage records the duration it measures, and ``process_intent``\npersists one ``dispatch_timings`` row per intent thread via\n``SessionStore.record_dispatch_timings(**timings.to_fields())``.\n\nDesign notes:\n\n- The clock is injectable (default ``time.monotonic``) so tests get\n  deterministic durations without sleeping.\n- Only stages actually measured appear in ``to_fields()``; unmeasured stages\n  are simply absent (the store upsert leaves them NULL), which is the honest\n  representation — e.g. ``synthesize_first_token_ms`` cannot be measured until\n  the synthesize strand streams, and ``escalate_ms`` is absent for hot-path\n  dispatches.\n- ``percentiles`` uses the nearest-rank method (ceil(q/100 * n)), matching how\n  latency SLOs are typically reported.
